passage.title: Dinner Helper Cards
passage.text: Our group keeps a family photo calendar by the fridge. On Saturday morning, Noah checks it before dinner. The family photo calendar has name cards for setting the table and bringing cups for the day's job. Mom asks us to finish one home job before dinner. Sometimes we compare today's name card with yesterday's name card and notice one small change. We like the family photo calendar because it helps us share the jobs fairly.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the family photo calendar is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the family photo calendar?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the family photo calendar is by the fridge.

questions.prompt: When does Noah use the family photo calendar?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Noah uses it on Saturday morning.

questions.prompt: What does the family photo calendar have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the family photo calendar has name cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
